parentQuestion: Which cranial nerve would you test by asking a patient to look laterally (to the...

scaffolds.q: To look to the right, the right eye must turn outward (laterally).
scaffolds.explain: When you look right, your right eye abducts (turns outward) and your left eye adducts (turns inward toward the nose). coordinated movement using different muscles in each eye. Precision choreography.

scaffolds.q: Abduction means movement away from the midline.
scaffolds.explain: Abduction moves a body part away from the midline. For the eye, this means turning outward (laterally), away from the nose. Feel the weight of this:the logic — the opposite is adduction, moving toward the midline.
